title: Amgen vs. Iovance Biotherapeutics: Which Healthcare Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Sara Appino
description: The article compares Amgen, an established pharmaceutical giant with $36.7B in revenue and strong profitability, against Iovance Biotherapeutics, an emerging biotech firm with innovative cancer therapies showing 60.6% revenue growth but still unprofitable. The author recommends Amgen for 2026 due to its stability, dividend, and proven track record, while acknowledging Iovance's compelling science but noting its execution risks and negative cash flow.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Established market leader with $36.7B revenue, 21% net margin, strong free cash flow of $8.1B, 16 brands growing double-digit rates, consistent dividend yield of 2.84%, and proven ability to navigate biosimilar competition. Author explicitly recommends it as the 'steadier ship' for 2026.

Shows impressive 60.6% revenue growth and innovative TIL therapy technology with compelling science, but faces significant headwinds: unprofitable with -148.4% net margin, negative free cash flow of -$336.2M, recent quarterly revenue miss, and high execution risk in manufacturing and market adoption. Author acknowledges potential but deems it unsuitable for long-term holds without higher risk tolerance.

title: Cutaneous Lupus Erythematosus Market to Expand at a CAGR of 8% During the Forecast Period (2026–2036) Amid Emerging Therapies and Increasing Healthcare Investments | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The cutaneous lupus erythematosus (CLE) market is projected to grow at 8% CAGR through 2036, driven by emerging targeted therapies and increased disease awareness. The market was valued at USD 650 million in 2025 across seven major markets. Key pipeline drugs from major pharmaceutical companies targeting plasmacytoid dendritic cells and type I interferon pathways are expected to transform treatment options for CLE patients.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Litifilimab (BIIB059) met primary endpoint in Phase II AMETHYST trial and received FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation in January 2026, positioning the company favorably in the emerging CLE treatment landscape.

Daxdilimab demonstrated positive Phase II results in discoid lupus erythematosus with early enrollment completion and advancement to Phase III development planned, indicating strong pipeline progress.
SOTYKTU (deucravacitinib) is in the clinical pipeline for CLE treatment, contributing to the company's presence in the growing autoimmune dermatology market.
SOTYKTU (deucravacitinib) is in the clinical pipeline for CLE treatment, contributing to the company's presence in the growing autoimmune dermatology market.
Anifrolumab (SAPHNELO) is approved for SLE and being evaluated for cutaneous lupus erythematosus, expanding therapeutic applications in the lupus market.
IMVT-1402 is mentioned as a pipeline candidate for CLE, but limited clinical data or development updates provided in the article.

title: Amgen Seeks FDA Hearing To Challenge Proposed Tavneos Withdrawal
author: Vandana Singh
description: Amgen has formally requested an FDA hearing to challenge the proposed withdrawal of Tavneos (avacopan), a drug for treating ANCA-associated vasculitis. The company argues that the drug's benefits continue to outweigh its risks and plans to submit detailed data by June 29, 2026. An independent review of the pivotal clinical trial data by Duke Clinical Research Institute is currently underway.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company faces a proposed FDA withdrawal of Tavneos, a significant product. While Amgen is actively defending the drug's approval, the regulatory challenge and ongoing independent review of clinical trial data represent material risks to the product's market availability and company revenue. Stock was down 0.05% at time of publication.

title: AbbVie Reports Promising New Clinical Updates. Here's What It Means for the Company's Dividend.
author: Jeff Siegel
description: AbbVie presented 21 clinical presentations on blood cancer therapies at the European Hematology Association Congress, showcasing progress in its oncology portfolio which generated $6.7 billion in 2025 revenue. The company's diversified pipeline across immunology, oncology, and neuroscience is helping offset Humira's patent-driven decline and could support future dividend growth through expanded cash flow.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Positive clinical updates on 21 blood cancer presentations, diversified revenue streams reducing single-product dependency, and strong oncology portfolio ($6.7B revenue) supporting future dividend sustainability and growth potential.
Mentioned only as a comparison point for dividend yield (roughly 2% vs AbbVie's 3%); no specific news or analysis provided about the company.

title: President Trump Loaded Up on Eli Lilly in Q1. Should You Buy the Obesity Drug Stock Too?
author: Jeff Siegel
description: President Trump's Q1 2026 financial disclosures revealed significant purchases of Eli Lilly stock, a major player in the booming anti-obesity drug market. The article cautions investors against simply copying political trades, emphasizing that investment decisions should be based on business fundamentals rather than celebrity or political endorsements. Eli Lilly's obesity treatments Mounjaro and Zepbound generated over $30 billion in combined sales in 2025, with analysts projecting the global anti-obesity drug market could exceed $95-200 billion. While Lilly maintains a strong competitive position with manufacturing advantages and a robust pipeline, its premium valuation already reflects years of future growth, presenting significant downside risk if sales slow or competition intensifies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Dominates the fast-growing anti-obesity drug market with $30B+ in combined sales from Mounjaro and Zepbound in 2025. Strong manufacturing capacity expansion, deep pipeline, substantial financial resources, and projected earnings growth from $23 to $37-52 per share over four years support positive outlook. However, premium valuation and competitive threats temper enthusiasm.

Co-dominates the anti-obesity market alongside Eli Lilly but faces stronger competition from Lilly's expanding manufacturing capacity and market share gains. Continues to expand its anti-obesity franchise but growth trajectory appears weaker than Lilly's.
Mentioned as one of several competitors developing next-generation obesity treatments, representing increasing competitive pressure in the market but without specific performance metrics provided.
Listed among large pharmaceutical firms developing next-generation obesity treatments, representing competitive threat to Eli Lilly's market dominance.
Identified as a competitor developing obesity treatments, contributing to increasing competitive pressure in the anti-obesity drug space.
